Research reactor at Rossendorf
Thermal neutron beam
.A 18 cm3 Ge(Li) detector with a resolution of 2.0 keV
at 200 keV was used in the energy range 50 keV to 1500
keV.
.A 27 cm3 Ge(Li) detector with a resolution of 5.5 keV
at 5 MeV was used for the higher energies.
.In addition, a high resolution planar Ge(Li) detector
(0.4 cm3, resolution = 0.5 keV at 100 keV) was used in
the energy range 40 keV to 300 keV
Except for the planar detector, a 0.5 cm thick
Li6-F shield was used against scattered neutrons.
Thermal capture gamma-rays.

The uncertainty on the relative intensities are from 10
to 15% for strong transitions and up to 100% for weak
transitions.

5.5 g/cm2 enriched (90.3%) W-184 in metallic powder
In order to establish a clear ordering of the observed
gamma-rays, the capture spectra for W-184, W-186 and
W-natural were measured under the same experimental
conditions.
Energy calibration was performed using the standard
calibration lines of the Co-60, Cs-137, Gd-153 and
ra-226 decays.
Data taken from ZFK-260, Table 5.

5.5 g/cm2 enriched (90.3 %) W-184 in metallic powder
In order to establish a clear ordering of the observed
gamma-rays, the capture spectra for W-184, W-186 and
W-natural were measured under the same experimental
conditions.
The energy calibration was performed using the intense
lines from the high energy gamma-ray spectrum from the
W-186(n,g)W-187 reaction.

relative intensities.
0.5 g/cm2 enriched (96.9%) W-186 in metallic powder
In order to establish a clear ordering of the observed
gamma-rays, the capture spectra for W-184, W-186 and
W-natural were measured under the same experimental
conditions.
Energy calibration was performed using the standard
calibration lines of the Co-60, Cs-137, Gd-153 and
Ra-226 decays.
1. The line at 59.2 keV superimposes with the K-alpha
x-rays of tungsten, therefore no reliable intensity
could be deduced.
2. The spectrum of conversion electrons was measured
and is also given in ZFK-260 as well as conversion
factors and multipolarities of a few transitions.
Data are from ZFK-260, Table 1.

0.5 g/cm2 enriched (96.9%) W-186 in metallic powder.
In order to establish a clear ordering of the observed
gamma-rays, the capture spectra for W-184, W-186 and
W-natural were measured under the same experimental
conditions.
The energy calibration was performed using known back-
ground transitions from (n,g) reactions on Al, Fe and H
as well as a Cl-35(n,g)Cl-36 calibration spectrum.
The level-energies were calculated using
Data are from ZFK-260, Table 2.
